name: Multi-agent RL framework (JaxMARL / PettingZoo + MAPPO/IPPO)
role: Trains the HIGH-LEVEL coordination policy on the kinematic env using CTDE (centralized training, decentralized execution). Output is a shared-parameter policy whose actions are setpoints (waypoint/velocity/heading) — the 'one continuous model' that anticipates and avoids other cars.
decision: Add explicitly. Use MAPPO or IPPO: a centralized critic sees global state (all cars) at train time; each car acts from local obs at run time. Pair with the JAX kinematic env -> JaxMARL (native JAX, fast). The trained policy is later run FROZEN on top of the low-level controller in Isaac.
outputs: trained decentralized coordination policy (shared weights, setpoint actions)
learning curve: crash-rate down / throughput up
design_notes: Non-stationarity (env changes as others learn) is THE multi-agent challenge -> CTDE is the standard answer.
No separate 'predict the other car' model needed: centralized critic + others' states in the obs -> anticipation learned implicitly (= the doc's 'one continuous model, not two entities').
Shared-parameter homogeneous agents -> scales to many cars cheaply.
Action space = setpoints, so the SAME policy drives both the kinematic env (training) and the Isaac demo (via the frozen low-level controller).
Optional: artificial-potential-field reward shaping as an early-learning safety prior.
alternatives: MetaDrive/SMARTS built-in MARL if you use their env instead of the custom kinematic one
risks: reward hacking (idle-forever, circle-driving) -> shape carefully (see reward-system)
instability with many simultaneously-learning agents -> curriculum, start with few cars
